notes: In Latin America 'vos' is used instead of 'tú' in some countries like Argentina and Uruguay. Avoid Spain-specific slang when targeting Latin American audiences.
Arabic is written right-to-left. Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) is used in formal writing; spoken dialects vary greatly by region. Egyptian dialect is the most widely understood.
common_mistakes: Using MSA in casual conversation sounds stiff
Dialects are not mutually intelligible across all regions

notes: Simplified characters are used in mainland China; Traditional in Taiwan and Hong Kong. Tones are critical — the same syllable with different tones has completely different meanings.
